skills: current-events vocabulary (COVID-19 vaccine names, antigen test)
recipe/label comprehension with fraction vocabulary in words (a quarter, a half)
picture true-statement selection (community service / road cleaning)
exclamation reaction to an accidental event
holiday identification from a descriptive clue without naming it (decorated eggs -> Easter)
seasonal inference from an activity clue (sunbathing at the beach)
exclusion-logic distractor (restaurant serves only Thai/Asian food -> want the one non-Asian option)
superlative pie-chart selection matching a stated superlative fact
map reading with inline picture icons embedded in the text (post office/shopping-cart icons)
diary reading comprehension (pandemic-era gardening) - main idea, precise multi-item detail matching, reason inference
dialogue completion across many everyday settings (16 items, items 17-32)
grammar: fraction/quantifier vocabulary (a quarter of, half a cup)
question_types: map reading with inline pictorial icons
diary reading comprehension with 4 linked questions
short 2-line dialogue completion across many settings (16 items)
reading_patterns: first-person pandemic-era diary (home gardening with father): main idea, precise identification of which items were planted (distinguishing materials used from crops grown), reason-inference for a stated emotion (pride)
dialogue_patterns: phone-call unavailability exchange (may I speak to X -> she's not in)
sympathy-for-failure exchange (crying because failed a test)
future-time-reference matching (when are we meeting -> future answer only)
advice-with-reason exchange (needs suntan lotion because it's sunny)
yes/no-polarity-consistency exchange (did you have a good time -> yes+positive elaboration)
favourite-thing exchange matching the correct answer category (a sport name, not equipment)
distractor_patterns: exclusion-logic trap (the only non-Asian dish is the answer when "only Thai/Asian food" is served)
wrong-emotion-valence reply (mismatched register to a sad/emergency cue)
material-vs-product confusion in reading comprehension (bamboo used AS MATERIAL vs the crops actually grown)
swapped superlative direction in pie-chart labels
answer_logic: current-events items require up-to-date general knowledge vocabulary rather than pure grammar (reflecting the COVID-19 pandemic period)
reading comprehension items require distinguishing between a material/tool mentioned and the actual subject/product being described
procedural/sequential items require matching a multi-step real-world process (e.g. mask disposal) to text/pictures in the correct order
exclusion-based dialogue items require identifying the one option that falls outside a category explicitly ruled out in the prompt
notes: Exam sat 12 Feb 2565 (BE+1 sitting-date convention); file name lacks the dot in 'ป6' unlike other years but refers to academic year 2564. Only 32 items total (items 1-16 standalone/reading, items 17-32 dialogue) — first year with a reduced 32-item format instead of 40; this shorter format persists through 2568. Strongly pandemic-themed (vaccines, home gardening, mask hygiene).
